title: Kubernetes Pod-Security-Standards admission configuration
description: Per-namespace Kubernetes Pod-Security-Standards (PSS) admission configuration captured read-only from the Kubernetes API (get/list on core namespaces — a rule the base connector already holds): which namespaces carry the pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce, /audit, and /warn labels and at which level (privileged / baseline / restricted), plus the optional pinned version label per mode. This is the ENFORCED side of workload hardening (does admission block / audit / warn on under-hardened pods), complementing the k8s.workload_security_context.v1 ACTUAL-posture kind. The minimum that demonstrates admission-time enforcement of the configuration-baseline control (e.g. which namespaces enforce a restricted PSS vs are unenforced). PSS LABEL configuration ONLY — never pod specs, container env, Secret / ConfigMap values, logs, nor any other namespace label or annotation beyond the pod-security.kubernetes.io/* labels (the connector reads ONLY those label keys; a structural reflection guard fails the build if a record field for other namespace metadata is added). Scope: namespace PSS labels only; this does NOT read the cluster's AdmissionConfiguration file (out of API reach), validating/mutating webhooks, or third-party policy engines (OPA/Gatekeeper, Kyverno) — those are documented follow-ons. The connector emits a descriptive PASS/FAIL verdict per namespace (PASS = enforces baseline/restricted; FAIL = unenforced or privileged-only); the platform evaluator owns the final policy call. x-default-scf-anchors are default mapping hints flagged for maintainer recheck (OQ #9).

namespace.description: Namespace the PSS admission assessment is for.

configured.description: True when at least one PSS mode (enforce/audit/warn) carries a level on this namespace — i.e. PSS admission is configured at all. False means the namespace has no pod-security.kubernetes.io/* labels (unenforced).

enforce_level.description: The pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce level. Omitted when the namespace has no enforce label (admission is not blocked). 'restricted'/'baseline' is hardened; 'privileged' is unrestricted.

enforce_version.description: The pinned pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version label (a kube minor like 'v1.29', or 'latest'). Omitted when absent.

audit_level.description: The pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit level (logs a violation, does not block). Omitted when absent.

audit_version.description: The pinned pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit-version label. Omitted when absent.

warn_level.description: The pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn level (returns a user-facing warning, does not block). Omitted when absent.

warn_version.description: The pinned pod-security.kubernetes.io/warn-version label. Omitted when absent.
